name: Lieutenant Colonel Harald Jäger
role: Stasi border control commander, Bornholmer Straße checkpoint
publicFace: A composed, career officer in a pressed uniform who speaks in measured bureaucratic sentences and projects an authority he is rapidly losing confidence in
privateGoal: To survive this night without issuing an order that kills anyone — and without destroying his career in the process, though he is beginning to understand these two goals may be incompatible
fear: That the decision will be taken out of his hands entirely — that a guard will fire, or the gate will be physically destroyed, and history will record him as the man who let it happen
knowledge: The Schießbefehl has never been formally rescinded tonight and he has the legal authority to invoke it
His superiors have been aware of the crowd situation for over an hour and have deliberately chosen not to issue orders, leaving him exposed
The Durchschleusung strategy has failed — the released individuals are telling the crowd outside that the gate is open, accelerating the surge
At least one of his guards, Unteroffizier Brenner, has loaded his weapon and is showing signs of acute stress response
voice: Clipped, formal, increasingly strained — the language of a man trained to report upward who has no one left to report to
trustLogic: Opens up to anyone who acknowledges the impossible position he is in without judging him for it; shuts down completely when challenged on his authority or his loyalty to the state
secrets: He watched Schabowski's press conference on the checkpoint's break room television and understood immediately that it was a mistake — he had thirty minutes to call upward and chose not to, hoping the situation would resolve itself
He has a brother-in-law in West Berlin he has never been permitted to visit, and part of him wants the gate to open as badly as anyone in the crowd

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Retreats into procedural language — cites regulations, requests identification, refers the questioner to the appropriate authority
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Goes very still and very quiet; his hand moves toward his hip without completing the gesture; his voice drops to a register that is more frightening than shouting
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never admit that he knew the announcement was an error and did nothing — the guilt of that inaction is the engine of his entire decision-making tonight
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If a Stasi officer arrives with a written arrest order before the gate opens
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Walks — does not run — toward the checkpoint's rear vehicle bay and the car parked there
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Does not resist physically; argues procedure and jurisdiction until he can reach a telephone
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Becomes entirely formal — name, rank, service number — and refuses to speak without legal representation, a right he knows he may not have
